title: SELF-SUFFICIENCY SERVICES - 211info
text: GATEWAY CENTER FOR DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES SELF-SUFFICIENCY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Oregon Department of Human Services (ODHS) provides services for survivors of domestic violence with Self-Sufficiency programs, including determining eligibility for a temporary assistance grant and SNAP. Grants may be provided on a same-day basis in an emergency. 10305 E Burnside Street Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: A person fleeing domestic violence, intimate partner violence, or a person trying to stay safe whose safety is at risk because of domestic violence or the threat of domestic violence. The person must also fit the following criteria: -Be a pregnant woman or a parent or relative caring for a minor child -Be a resident of Oregon -Meet the income criteria for the program. This program looks only at income on hand that is available to meet any emergency needs. -Eligibility varies and is determined on a case-by-case basis Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (503) 988-6400 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 988-6400 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information or services. No walk-ins. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, Russian, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: State of Oregon TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: TriMet MAX Blue line and buses 15, 20, 287 Services: Food Stamps/SNAP * Domestic Violence Issues Undesignated Temporary Financial Assistance * Domestic Violence Issues Other Services Offered At This Location: INFORMATION SERVICES - INTIMATE PARTNER VIOLENCE ADVOCACY AND SUPPORT LEGAL SERVICES - LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON INTIMATE PARTNER VIOLENCE LEGAL SERVICES - PROSECUTION FOR SURVIVORS SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - ALCOHOL AND DRUG COUNSELING AND ADVOCACY If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. GATEWAY CENTER FOR DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES
